title: fagelia bicolor
intro: Fagelia bicolor, also known as the "Wild Fagelia," is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Boraginaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Fagelia bicolor include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Fagelia bicolor is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be made into a poultice or salve for topical application.
